+## S3 UNLOCK STATUS — IMPOSSIBLE UNTIL T3 R&D
+### Core Issue: S3 quality requirements are VERY HIGH even at cheap prices
+- 104/108 S3 leads LOST this week despite Plan A quality 0.2578 at just $12

+- S3 Info L1 q_min center: 0.145 (±65%) BUT empirically most S3 need quality >> 0.2578
+- **Plan C at $140 had 0% conversion** — quality 0.3093 is insufficient
+
+### Why S3 Chose Plan A Not Plan C:
+- Plan A utility for S3: 0.2578 × (1-12/193) = **0.242** ← higher
+- Plan C utility at $140 for S3: 0.3093 × (1-140/193) = **0.085** ← way lower
+- S3 will ALWAYS prefer Plan A over Plan C at high prices (price eats utility)
+- To make Plan C preferred: need price < $22 (same utility as Plan A at $12) OR quality >> 0.40+
+
+### Plan C Minimum Profitable Price:
+- S3 uses 570 units/day (Info L1). Compute at T3: 570 × $0.006 × 30 = **$102.60/month**
+- Break-even price for Plan C T3 with S3 usage: **$103/month minimum**
+- Current Plan C $140: profitable at +$37.4/month margin per S3 sub ✓
+- **DO NOT LOWER Plan C below $103** — would lose money on S3 subscribers
+- The $140 pricing is CORRECT for future use when quality is high enough
+
+### Path to S3 unlock:
+- Need quality >> 0.31 for Plan C at prices > $103 where S3 would prefer it over Plan A
+- T3 R&D (+0.11 mean boost) brings quality to ~0.41 → then need Plan A to be less attractive
+- After T3: Plan A T1 downgrade OR pricing restructure to steer S3 to Plan C
+
+## T3 R&D STATUS
